response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced engineer with strong testing culture, mentoring activity, and some high-throughput systems exposure, but he falls well short of the role's core minimum qualifications. The position requires 8 years of C++ experience; his C++ work amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments plus a thesis project, with most of his systems work in DLang, TypeScript, and Node.js. His infrastructure experience is meaningful (low-latency microservices at millions of requests/second, Kubernetes deployments) but is closer to 2-3 years than the required 5, and he has no formal technical-leadership role on a cloud compute platform.
strengths: Real low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong mentoring and knowledge-sharing track record (workshops, courses, refactoring dojos)
Solid engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and open-source tooling authorship
concerns: Far below the 8-year C++ requirement — only ~6 months of professional C/C++ work
Limited large-scale infrastructure and cloud platform depth relative to the 5-year requirement
No formal technical-leadership experience managing priorities, deadlines, and cross-team alignment
